purpose: A light-DOM element wrapping a real <button>: renders the token-driven skeleton, reflects variants into classes, forwards native semantics, and adds the one behavior a native button lacks (loading).
useWhen: The user triggers an action: submit, confirm, open, dismiss, toggle a panel.
Work is in flight after activation — `loading` announces it (aria-busy), suppresses re-activation, and keeps the button focusable.

notWhen.instead: An <a> element — buttons act, links navigate.

notWhen.case: The control flips a persistent on/off setting.

guidance: `type` defaults to "button" — the platform's "submit" default is a footgun inside forms; opt into submit explicitly.
Prefer `loading` over `disabled` for in-flight work: unlike disabled, it keeps the button focusable so keyboard and screen-reader users don't lose their place mid-operation.
Style variants (ghost, outline, subtle) share one axis and are alternatives; size variants (sm, lg) compose with them: variant="ghost sm".

default.description: The button's label content (its accessible name).
guidance: Icon-only buttons must supply the accessible name via aria-label or aria-labelledby; dev mode warns when the computed name is empty.

icon.description: A supplementary pictogram rendered aria-hidden beside the label — any markup: svg, icon-font glyph, HTML character, image.
guidance: Use data-position="start|end" for explicit placement; when omitted, placement is inferred from authored child order.
Icons inherit the label's ink (currentColor); there is no separate icon color token.
The icon is aria-hidden: it can reinforce the label, never replace it — meaning must live in the accessible name.

keyboard.does: Activates the button; arrives as a native click (native behavior, preserved). Suppressed while loading.

examples.title: Explicit submit in a form
examples.why: type must opt into "submit" — zebkit defaults it to "button".

examples.title: Icon with label
examples.html: <zbk-button variant="outline"><svg slot="icon" data-position="start" viewBox="0 0 16 16"><path d="M8 0 16 16H0z"/></svg>Save draft</zbk-button>

examples.title: Composed variants
examples.why: ghost (style axis) and sm (size axis) compose; two style-axis variants would conflict and warn.

examples.title: Loading
examples.why: aria-busy announces the in-flight work; activation is suppressed but focus is kept.

examples.title: Icon-only
examples.why: Valid only with an aria-label supplying the accessible name; without one, dev mode warns.

examples.title: Button as navigation
examples.why: Navigation belongs to <a>: middle-click, open-in-new-tab, and link semantics for AT are all lost.
